methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $110M last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $150M - $1.8B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Watershed
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $1.8B

private.name: Graphyte
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M

private.name: Fourth Power
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $150M

data.narrative:
Steady Energy, a Series A climate and energy company founded by Tommi Nyman, is evaluated against a comparable universe of 3 same-sector, same-stage private companies plus 1 public comp (LNZA). The target's last post-money valuation stands at $110M on €32M raised to date.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression modeling, using a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical for Series A advancement, implies $220M. Peer-median analysis across three direct comparables—Watershed ($1.8B), Graphyte ($200M), and Fourth Power ($150M)—yields a median of $200M, with a range of $150M to $1.8B. The wide spread reflects heterogeneity in the climate-tech subsector; the median is anchored by Graphyte and Fourth Power.

The two methods triangulate to a range of $170M (low) to $253M (high), with a median of $210M. This implies modest upside from the current $110M post-money, consistent with typical Series A mark-to-market dynamics. Key uncertainties include the appropriate weighting of Watershed—a substantial outlier at $1.8B—and whether stage-progression multiples will hold given sector volatility in climate and energy deployment economics.
